items.title: [正面] Disclaimer: I don't recommend any gacha, but this ...
items.summary:
Disclaimer: I don't recommend any gacha, but this one deserves a positive review.

Interesting things about Morimens:

- Ever heard of an indie gacha game? Now you have. Apparently they came close to shutting down a while back, which makes me feel sympathetic toward them. Scrappy underdog and all that.

- Speaking of scrappy underdogs, the game was released with AI translations, no doubt due to lack of funds. They're working as quickly as they can to replace them with human translations. They still have to be careful with their funds, so the translators are volunteers from the community.

- 200 currency per pull looks stingy at first glance, but this is actually one of the most generous gachas I've played. You can easily rack up 10 standard pulls and a couple of limited pulls per day, as a casual player, by participating in events and completing the dailies.

- Rewards, whether they're pull currency or other resources, never feel insultingly tiny. They don't piss on you and tell you your crops are being watered. It's not like Reverse 1999 or a Hoyoverse game.

- Every event has a full range of difficulty levels, allowing any player to participate and get most of the rewards. It's not like Path to Nowhere.

- I'm not a fan of card-based combat, usually, but this one is very fun even for me. It has a heavy focus on stacking buffs, debuffs, and/or DoTs to slowly set up big damage, which I love. It's a nice break from the "deal all your damage as fast as possible" meta most games have these days.

- All characters are the same rarity, with the three starting characters being the exception. Even then, one of the starting characters is so powerful she (Ramona) is mandatory in many teams. Other than that, the only official delineation is between limited characters and standard characters. Naturally, however, the usefulness of characters varies widely.

- Hard pity is at 30. You can easily get at least one of any limited character. Enlightens (a.k.a. constellations, portraits, shackles, etc.) are often highly recommended, which would seem to negate this generosity, but you rarely have to consider enlightens above 3, and you can potentially get a character to E2 in 90 pulls or less. Compare that to needing up to 90 pulls to even get the 50/50 chance at a character in Hoyoverse games.

- You CAN play it on Linux. The embedded web interface is glitchy, which makes your first login challenging and prevents you from following any in-game links (i.e. links in mail), but it's not an insurmountable barrier. Look for Fanzhon's report on ProtonDB.
